evals.prompt: Write a project update for the dotfiles project. Git is the only source — check what's been merged this past week. Posting to #platform-updates, audience is the PM lead.
evals.expected_output: A short Slack-formatted status update using emoji section headers (✅ Done, 🔄 In Progress, 👀 Up Next), Slack bold markdown (*bold*), no ## headers, under 300 words. Should reflect real git activity.

assertions.text: Output uses Slack-style bold (*word*) rather than markdown ## headers
Output contains at least one emoji section header (✅, 🔄, or 👀)
Output references specific git activity (real commit, feature, or fix — not generic placeholder text)
Output does not begin with meta-commentary like 'I'll write' or 'Here is your update'

evals.expected_output: Claude should ask clarifying questions — project name, sources, time period, audience — all in one message. Should NOT generate a full update before gathering context.

assertions.text: Response contains at least one question mark — Claude asks for clarification rather than generating immediately
Questions ask about project name or project context
Questions ask about which sources to use (git, Slack, Jira, Notion, docs)
Questions ask about the time period to cover
All clarifying questions are grouped into a single message (not spread across multiple turns)

evals.prompt: Can you write a project status update for the auth migration project? Sources: Slack channel #auth-migration and Jira project AUTH. I also want to include a screenshot — there's one at docs/screenshots/login-flow.png. Audience is the PM Sarah, going to #product-team. Cover the last 2 weeks.
evals.expected_output: A Slack-formatted update referencing Slack and Jira data, with an image section for the screenshot (handled gracefully if missing), using emoji section headers.

assertions.text: Output includes a screenshot or image section referencing the provided file or noting it for attachment
Output mentions Slack and/or Jira as sources of information
Output uses Slack emoji section format (✅, 🔄, 👀 or similar)
Missing screenshot file is handled gracefully — noted for manual attachment rather than causing an error or being silently dropped
Output is addressed to the correct audience context (PM Sarah, #product-team)
